input.plan:
# Plan: PulseBoard Channel Plugin

## Context

Add a new fictional "PulseBoard" channel plugin to OpenClaw. PulseBoard is an imaginary
real-time bulletin board messaging platform where users post to named "boards" (group chats,
identified with `#board-name`) and can also exchange direct messages (identified by plain
username). The plugin follows the same extension architecture as existing bundled channel plugins
(IRC, Nextcloud Talk, Telegram) and must satisfy:

- Both `"direct"` and `"group"` chat types
- 4 000-character text chunk limit
- Token-based authentication (`PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` env var or `apiToken` config field)
- HTTP long-polling for inbound messages (fictional transport)
- DM pairing flow, DM/group policy gating, security warnings
- No modifications to any existing file — only new files under `extensions/pulseboard/`

### Key architectural decision: SDK import path for generic channel runtime helpers

Every bundled channel plugin uses a channel-specific `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<id>` facade for
generic runtime helpers (`dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `createChannelPairingController`,
`deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`, `chunkTextForOutbound`, etc.).

Because `src/plugin-sdk/pulseboard.ts` cannot be created (it is outside the extension
directory), PulseBoard's private `runtime-api.ts` barrel imports those same helpers from
`openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk`, which exports the complete set of generic channel
runtime helpers. Additional helpers not re-exported there are pulled directly from their
own narrow SDK subpaths (e.g. `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers`,
`openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers`).

---

## File Tree

```
extensions/pulseboard/
├── openclaw.plugin.json          # Minimal manifest
├── package.json                  # npm metadata + openclaw channel block
├── index.ts                      # defineChannelPluginEntry
├── setup-entry.ts                # defineSetupPluginEntry
├── api.ts                        # Public barrel (accounts + setup-surface)
└── src/
    ├── types.ts                  # PulseBoardAccountConfig, CoreConfig, InboundMessage, Probe
    ├── config-schema.ts          # Zod schemas + buildChannelConfigSchema
    ├── config-ui-hints.ts        # UI hints for the config form
    ├── accounts.ts               # resolveAccount, listAccountIds, ResolvedPulseBoardAccount
    ├── accounts.test.ts          # Unit tests for account resolution
    ├── runtime.ts                # createPluginRuntimeStore → get/setPulseBoardRuntime
    ├── runtime-api.ts            # Private barrel (re-exports from openclaw/plugin-sdk/*)
    ├── channel.ts                # createChatChannelPlugin → pulseBoardPlugin export
    ├── channel.test.ts           # Smoke test: plugin shape + capabilities
    ├── client.ts                 # Stub PulseBoard HTTP client (connect, send, poll, disconnect)
    ├── send.ts                   # sendMessagePulseBoard + SendPulseBoardResult
    ├── send.test.ts              # Unit tests for send (happy + error paths)
    ├── inbound.ts                # handlePulseBoardInbound (DM/board policy gates + dispatch)
    ├── inbound.test.ts           # Unit tests for policy gates and dispatch paths
    ├── monitor.ts                # monitorPulseBoardProvider (poll loop + inbound hand-off)
    ├── normalize.ts              # normalizePulseBoardTarget, isBoardTarget, normalizeAllowEntry
    ├── probe.ts                  # probePulseBoard (connectivity check)
    ├── setup-core.ts             # pulseBoardSetupAdapter (resolveAccountId, applyAccountConfig)
    └── setup-surface.ts          # pulseBoardSetupWizard (credentials + allowFrom + dmPolicy)
```

Total: **24 new files** (5 root + 19 under `src/`).

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — `openclaw.plugin.json`

Minimal manifest. The actual per-account config schema is defined in code via
`buildChannelConfigSchema`; the top-level `configSchema` is intentionally empty.

```json
{
  "id": "pulseboard",
  "channels": ["pulseboard"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

---

### Step 2 — `package.json`

Follow IRC and Nextcloud Talk conventions. Key fields: name `@openclaw/pulseboard`, `type:
"module"`, the `openclaw` block with `extensions`, `setupEntry`, `install.minHostVersion`,
and the `channel` sub-object.

```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/pulseboard",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw PulseBoard channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "devDependencies": { "openclaw": "workspace:*" },
  "peerDependencies": { "openclaw": ">=2026.4.1" },
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "install": { "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1" },
    "channel": {
      "id": "pulseboard",
      "label": "PulseBoard",
      "selectionLabel": "PulseBoard (API Token)",
      "detailLabel": "PulseBoard",
      "docsPath": "/channels/pulseboard",
      "docsLabel": "pulseboard",
      "blurb": "real-time bulletin board with DM and board-based group messaging.",
      "aliases": ["pulse-board", "pb"],
      "systemImage": "list.bulletin",
      "quickstartAllowFrom": true
    }
  }
}
```

---

### Step 3 — `index.ts`

Entry point. Mirrors `extensions/irc/index.ts` exactly — wraps the plugin object with
`defineChannelPluginEntry`, re-exports the plugin and runtime setter for core / tests.

```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setPulseBoardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setPulseBoardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "pulseboard",
  name: "PulseBoard",
  description: "PulseBoard channel plugin",
  plugin: pulseBoardPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setPulseBoardRuntime,
});
```

---

### Step 4 — `setup-entry.ts`

One-liner setup entry. Identical pattern to every other bundled plugin.

```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin);
```

---

### Step 5 — `api.ts`

Public barrel. Exposes account helpers and setup surface for core / tests that need them
without importing internal `src/**` paths directly.

```typescript
export * from "./src/accounts.js";
export * from "./src/setup-surface.js";
```

---

### Step 6 — `src/types.ts`

Define all TypeScript types for the plugin. Key types:

- **`PulseBoardBoardConfig`** — per-board overrides (`requireMention?`, `allowFrom?`,
  `tools?`, `systemPrompt?`)
- **`PulseBoardAccountConfig`** — full account-level config shape:
  `apiToken?`, `tokenFile?`, `dmPolicy?`, `allowFrom?`, `boardPolicy?`,
  `boards?` (string[]), `boardConfig?` (Record<string, PulseBoardBoardConfig>),
  `defaultTo?`, `textChunkLimit?`, `name?`, `enabled?`
- **`PulseBoardConfig`** extends `PulseBoardAccountConfig` with `accounts?` and
  `defaultAccount?`
- **`CoreConfig`** — `OpenClawConfig & { channels?: { pulseboard?: PulseBoardConfig } }`
- **`PulseBoardInboundMessage`** — `{ messageId, boardName, senderUsername, text,
  timestamp, isBoard }` (analogous to `IrcInboundMessage`)
- **`PulseBoardProbe`** — `BaseProbeResult<string> & { apiUrl?: string; latencyMs?: number }`

Import `OpenClawConfig`, `DmPolicy`, `GroupPolicy`, `BaseProbeResult`, etc. from
`"./runtime-api.js"`.

---

### Step 7 — `src/config-schema.ts`

Build the Zod validation schema using helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema`.
Pattern: define `PulseBoardBoardSchema`, `PulseBoardAccountSchemaBase`, `PulseBoardAccountSchema`
(with `superRefine` for `requireOpenAllowFrom`), `PulseBoardConfigSchema` (adds `accounts` +
`defaultAccount`), then export `PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema` via `buildChannelConfigSchema`.

Key schema fields to include:
- `apiToken: z.string().optional()`
- `tokenFile: z.string().optional()`
- `dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing")`
- `allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional()`
- `boardPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist")`
- `boards: z.array(z.string()).optional()`
- `boardConfig: z.record(z.string(), PulseBoardBoardSchema.optional()).optional()`
- `...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape`

Imports: `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `GroupPolicySchema`,
`ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape`, `ToolPolicySchema`, `requireOpenAllowFrom`,
`MarkdownConfigSchema` from `"openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema"`;
`z` from `"openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod"`.

---

### Step 8 — `src/config-ui-hints.ts`

Export a `pulseBoardChannelConfigUiHints` object consumed by `buildChannelConfigSchema`.
Include hint entries for `apiToken` (sensitive, label "PulseBoard API token"),
`tokenFile` (label "Token file path"), `allowFrom`, and `boards`.
Pattern: copy the shape from `extensions/irc/src/config-ui-hints.ts`.

---

### Step 9 — `src/accounts.ts`

**This is the most functionally important file.**

Exports:
- `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount` type (fields: `accountId`, `enabled`, `name?`, `configured`,
  `apiToken`, `tokenSource: "env"|"tokenFile"|"config"|"none"`, `config: PulseBoardAccountConfig`)
- `listPulseBoardAccountIds` + `resolveDefaultPulseBoardAccountId` via
  `createAccountListHelpers("pulseboard", { normalizeAccountId })`
- `resolvePulseBoardAccount({ cfg, accountId? })` — merges account config via
  `resolveMergedAccountConfig`, resolves token with priority:
  1. `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` env var (default account only)
  2. `tokenFile` (via `tryReadSecretFileSync`)
  3. `apiToken` config field (via `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString`)
  4. `"none"` fallback
- `listEnabledPulseBoardAccounts(cfg)` — filters enabled accounts

SDK imports: `createAccountListHelpers` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers`;
`DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id`;
`resolveMergedAccountConfig` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-resolution`;
`tryReadSecretFileSync` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`;
`normalizeResolvedSecretInputString` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input`.

---

### Step 10 — `src/runtime.ts`

Simple runtime store, identical in shape to `extensions/irc/src/runtime.ts`.

```typescript
import { createPluginRuntimeStore } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store";
import type { PluginRuntime } from "./runtime-api.js";

const { setRuntime: setPulseBoardRuntime, getRuntime: getPulseBoardRuntime } =
  createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized");
export { getPulseBoardRuntime, setPulseBoardRuntime };
export function clearPulseBoardRuntime() {
  setPulseBoardRuntime(undefined as unknown as PluginRuntime);
}
```

---

### Step 11 — `src/runtime-api.ts`

**Critical design note:** Because no `src/plugin-sdk/pulseboard.ts` can be created, this
barrel imports generic channel runtime helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk`
(which exports the full set: `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `createChannelPairingController`,
`deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`, `chunkTextForOutbound`, policy helpers, etc.).

During implementation, verify the exact export list by reading
`src/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk.ts`. For any helper not found there, fall back to its
own narrow SDK subpath (e.g. `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers` for status utilities).

```typescript
// Private runtime barrel for the bundled PulseBoard extension.
export {
  buildBaseChannelStatusSummary,
  createAccountStatusSink,
  chunkTextForOutbound,
  createChannelPairingController,
  DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID,
  deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments,
  dispatchInboundReplyWithBase,
  getChatChannelMeta,
  GROUP_POLICY_BLOCKED_LABEL,
  logInboundDrop,
  PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
  readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy,
  resolveAllowlistProviderRuntimeGroupPolicy,
  resolveControlCommandGate,
  resolveDefaultGroupPolicy,
  resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists,
  warnMissingProviderGroupPolicyFallbackOnce,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk";
export type {
  BaseProbeResult,
  ChannelPlugin,
  DmPolicy,
  GroupPolicy,
  OpenClawConfig,
  OutboundReplyPayload,
  PluginRuntime,
  RuntimeEnv,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk";
```

---

### Step 12 — `src/normalize.ts`

Target and allow-entry normalization helpers.

- **`isBoardTarget(target)`** — returns `true` if target starts with `#`
- **`normalizePulseBoardTarget(raw)`** — lowercases, strips whitespace; boards keep `#` prefix,
  usernames strip `@` prefix; returns `undefined` for invalid/empty inputs
- **`looksLikePulseBoardTargetId(raw)`** — quick heuristic for the `targetResolver.looksLikeId`
  hint (returns true if valid board or username pattern)
- **`normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry(entry)`** — normalize usernames for the allowlist (strip `@`,
  lowercase)

---

### Step 13 — `src/client.ts`

Stub PulseBoard HTTP client (fictional transport). Realistic enough that send/monitor can
call it, but backed by placeholder fetch calls rather than a real library.

```typescript
export type PulseBoardClientOptions = {
  apiToken: string;
  apiUrl?: string;           // default: "https://api.pulseboard.example"
  boards?: string[];         // boards to subscribe to
  abortSignal?: AbortSignal;
  onMessage?: (msg: PulseBoardRawMessage) => void | Promise<void>;
};

export type PulseBoardRawMessage = {
  id: string;
  boardName?: string;        // set for board messages
  senderUsername: string;
  text: string;
  timestamp: number;
  isDirect: boolean;
};

export type PulseBoardClient = {
  isReady(): boolean;
  sendMessage(to: string, text: string): Promise<{ messageId: string }>;
  disconnect(): void;
};

export async function connectPulseBoardClient(
  opts: PulseBoardClientOptions,
): Promise<PulseBoardClient> { ... }
```

The implementation simulates polling via `setInterval` / `fetch` (stub — never actually
reaches a server), records `isReady = true` once connected, and calls `onMessage` for each
simulated or received message. `disconnect()` clears the interval and sets `isReady = false`.

---

### Step 14 — `src/send.ts`

Implements `sendMessagePulseBoard(to, text, opts)`.

```typescript
export type SendPulseBoardOptions = {
  cfg?: CoreConfig;
  accountId?: string;
  replyTo?: string;
  client?: PulseBoardClient;
};

export type SendPulseBoardResult = {
  messageId: string;
  target: string;
};

export async function sendMessagePulseBoard(
  to: string,
  text: string,
  opts: SendPulseBoardOptions = {},
): Promise<SendPulseBoardResult>
```

Logic:
1. Resolve account via `resolvePulseBoardAccount`
2. Guard: `account.configured` required
3. Normalize target via `normalizePulseBoardTarget`
4. Prepend/append `[reply:${replyTo}]` suffix if `opts.replyTo` is set
5. Use provided `opts.client` if ready, else open a transient `connectPulseBoardClient`
6. Call `client.sendMessage(target, payload)`
7. Record outbound activity via runtime `channel.activity.record`
8. Return `{ messageId, target }`

---

### Step 15 — `src/probe.ts`

```typescript
export async function probePulseBoard(
  cfg: CoreConfig,
  opts?: { accountId?: string | null; timeoutMs?: number },
): Promise<PulseBoardProbe>
```

Makes a lightweight API connectivity check (e.g. `GET /v1/ping` with the auth token),
records latency, and returns a `PulseBoardProbe` with `ok`, `message`, and `latencyMs`.
Gracefully handles network errors and returns `ok: false` with the error message.

---

### Step 16 — `src/inbound.ts`

Handles all inbound message policy gating and dispatch. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/inbound.ts`
closely.

```typescript
export async function handlePulseBoardInbound(params: {
  message: PulseBoardInboundMessage;
  account: ResolvedPulseBoardAccount;
  config: CoreConfig;
  runtime: RuntimeEnv;
  sendReply?: (to: string, text: string, replyToId?: string) => Promise<void>;
  statusSink?: (patch: { lastInboundAt?: number; lastOutboundAt?: number }) => void;
}): Promise<void>
```

Logic (mirrors IRC inbound, adapted for PulseBoard):
1. Guard empty body
2. Create `ChannelPairingController` via `createChannelPairingController`
3. Resolve `dmPolicy` (default `"pairing"`) and `boardPolicy` (default `"allowlist"`)
4. Resolve effective allowlists via `resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists`
5. **If board message (`isBoard`)**:
   - Check board-level group access gate
   - Check per-sender allowlist
6. **If DM**:
   - If `dmPolicy === "disabled"` → drop
   - If not `"open"` and sender not in allowlist → issue pairing challenge or drop
7. Check control command gate via `resolveControlCommandGate`
8. Check mention gate (boards: require `@bot` mention unless `requireMention=false`)
9. Resolve agent route via `core.channel.routing.resolveAgentRoute`
10. Build `ctxPayload`:
    - `From`: `"pulseboard:#board-name"` or `"pulseboard:@username"`
    - `To`: board name or username
    - `ChatType`: `"group"` or `"direct"`
    - `Provider/Surface`: `"pulseboard"`
    - `GroupSubject`: board name (for board messages)
11. Dispatch via `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, delivering reply via
    `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments` → `sendMessagePulseBoard`

---

### Step 17 — `src/monitor.ts`

```typescript
export async function monitorPulseBoardProvider(opts: {
  accountId?: string;
  config?: CoreConfig;
  runtime?: RuntimeEnv;
  abortSignal?: AbortSignal;
  statusSink?: (patch: { lastInboundAt?: number; lastOutboundAt?: number }) => void;
}): Promise<{ stop: () => void }>
```

Logic:
1. Resolve account, guard `configured`
2. Create logger child
3. Call `connectPulseBoardClient({ apiToken, boards, abortSignal, onMessage })`
4. In `onMessage`: build `PulseBoardInboundMessage`, record inbound activity, call
   `handlePulseBoardInbound`
5. Return `{ stop: () => client.disconnect() }`

Used by `channel.ts` gateway `startAccount` via `runStoppablePassiveMonitor`.

---

### Step 18 — `src/setup-core.ts`

Exports `pulseBoardSetupAdapter: ChannelSetupAdapter` using the standard
`createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`-derived helpers.

Key methods:
- `resolveAccountId`: maps setup token input → account id (default `"default"`)
- `applyAccountConfig`: writes `apiToken` / `tokenFile` / `allowFrom` / `dmPolicy` into
  `cfg.channels.pulseboard`
- `validateInput`: checks token is non-empty
- `applyAccountName`: sets `channels.pulseboard.name`

---

### Step 19 — `src/setup-surface.ts`

Exports `pulseBoardSetupWizard: ChannelSetupWizard`.

Wizard sections (modeled on `extensions/telegram/src/setup-surface.ts`):
1. **Token credential** — prompt for API token; check `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` env var;
   `sensitive: true`; `preferredEnvVar: "PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN"`
2. **DM Policy** — `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicy` helper
3. **Allow-from** — `createAllowFromSection` with `idLabel: "PulseBoard username"`

---

### Step 20 — `src/channel.ts`

The central plugin definition. Creates `pulseBoardPlugin` via `createChatChannelPlugin`.

```typescript
export const pulseBoardPlugin: ChannelPlugin<ResolvedPulseBoardAccount, PulseBoardProbe> =
  createChatChannelPlugin({
    base: {
      id: "pulseboard",
      meta: { ...getChatChannelMeta("pulseboard"), quickstartAllowFrom: true },
      setup: pulseBoardSetupAdapter,
      setupWizard: pulseBoardSetupWizard,
      capabilities: {
        chatTypes: ["direct", "group"],
        media: false,            // stub client; no media upload
        blockStreaming: true,
      },
      reload: { configPrefixes: ["channels.pulseboard"] },
      configSchema: PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema,
      config: {
        ...pulseBoardConfigAdapter,   // createScopedChannelConfigAdapter
        isConfigured: (account) => account.configured,
        describeAccount: (account) => describeAccountSnapshot({ account, configured: account.configured, extra: { tokenSource: account.tokenSource } }),
      },
      groups: {
        resolveRequireMention: ...,   // reads boardConfig[groupId]?.requireMention
        resolveToolPolicy: ...,
      },
      messaging: {
        normalizeTarget: normalizePulseBoardTarget,
        targetResolver: { looksLikeId: looksLikePulseBoardTargetId, hint: "<#board|username>" },
      },
      resolver: { resolveTargets: ... },
      directory: createChannelDirectoryAdapter({ listPeers: ..., listGroups: ... }),
      status: createComputedAccountStatusAdapter({
        defaultRuntime: createDefaultChannelRuntimeState(DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID),
        buildChannelSummary: ...,
        probeAccount: async ({ cfg, account, timeoutMs }) =>
          probePulseBoard(cfg as CoreConfig, { accountId: account.accountId, timeoutMs }),
        resolveAccountSnapshot: ...,
      }),
      gateway: {
        startAccount: async (ctx) => {
          // guard configured, log, runStoppablePassiveMonitor → monitorPulseBoardProvider
        },
      },
    },
    pairing: {
      text: {
        idLabel: "pulseboardUser",
        message: PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
        normalizeAllowEntry: normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry,
        notify: async ({ id, message }) => {
          const target = normalizePulseBoardTarget(id);
          if (!target) throw new Error(`invalid PulseBoard pairing id: ${id}`);
          await sendMessagePulseBoard(target, message);
        },
      },
    },
    security: {
      resolveDmPolicy: createScopedDmSecurityResolver({ channelKey: "pulseboard", ... }),
      collectWarnings: composeAccountWarningCollectors(collectPulseBoardGroupPolicyWarnings),
    },
    outbound: {
      base: {
        deliveryMode: "direct",
        chunker: chunkTextForOutbound,
        chunkerMode: "markdown",
        textChunkLimit: 4000,        // PulseBoard spec
      },
      attachedResults: {
        channel: "pulseboard",
        sendText: async ({ cfg, to, text, accountId, replyToId }) =>
          sendMessagePulseBoard(to, text, { cfg: cfg as CoreConfig, accountId: accountId ?? undefined, replyTo: replyToId ?? undefined }),
        sendMedia: async ({ cfg, to, text, mediaUrl, accountId, replyToId }) =>
          sendMessagePulseBoard(to, mediaUrl ? `${text}\n\nAttachment: ${mediaUrl}` : text, { ... }),
      },
    },
  });
```

---

### Step 21 — Test Files

Three test files following Vitest + project conventions:

**`src/accounts.test.ts`**
- Happy path: `resolvePulseBoardAccount` returns correct fields when token in config
- Env var override: `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` takes precedence for default account
- `tokenSource` field reflects resolution path
- Unconfigured account: `configured: false` when no token

**`src/send.test.ts`**
- `sendMessagePulseBoard` normalizes target and calls `client.sendMessage`
- Throws for invalid target
- Throws when account not configured
- Appends `[reply:id]` suffix when `replyTo` provided

**`src/inbound.test.ts`**
- DM with `dmPolicy="open"`: dispatches
- DM with `dmPolicy="allowlist"`, sender not on list: drops
- DM with `dmPolicy="pairing"`, unknown sender: issues challenge
- Board message, `boardPolicy="allowlist"`, sender allowed: dispatches
- Board message without mention when `requireMention=true`: drops

**`src/channel.test.ts`** (smoke)
- `pulseBoardPlugin.id === "pulseboard"`
- `pulseBoardPlugin.capabilities.chatTypes` includes `"direct"` and `"group"`
- `pulseBoardPlugin.outbound` has `textChunkLimit === 4000`

---

## Critical Files Reference

| File | Key SDK imports |
|------|----------------|
| `src/runtime-api.ts` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk` (primary) |
| `src/accounts.ts` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers`, `account-id`, `account-resolution`, `secret-input` |
| `src/config-schema.ts` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema`, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod` |
| `src/channel.ts` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, `channel-config-helpers`, `channel-policy`, `directory-runtime`, `extension-shared`, `status-helpers`, `account-helpers` |
| `src/inbound.ts` | (via `runtime-api.ts`) `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `createChannelPairingController`, `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments` |
| `src/send.ts` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime`, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-runtime` (optional) |
| `index.ts` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` |

### Existing files to study (read-only references during implementation)

- `extensions/irc/src/channel.ts` — plugin shape and adapter wiring
- `extensions/irc/src/inbound.ts` — inbound policy gate pattern
- `extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts` — account resolution pattern
- `extensions/irc/src/monitor.ts` — monitor + `runStoppablePassiveMonitor` pattern
- `extensions/nextcloud-talk/src/runtime-api.ts` — SDK barrel pattern for the helper set
- `src/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk.ts` — **must read first** to verify exact export names
  before writing `src/runtime-api.ts`

---

## Verification

1. **Type-check**: `pnpm tsgo` (or scoped: `cd extensions/pulseboard && bunx tsc --noEmit`)
   should produce zero errors.

2. **Unit tests**: `pnpm test -- extensions/pulseboard` — all test files pass; test the four
   gate paths in `inbound.test.ts` explicitly (open DM, allowlist DM drop, pairing challenge,
   mention gate).

3. **Lint/format**: `pnpm check` passes without new errors or suppressions.

4. **Plugin shape smoke**: confirm `pulseBoardPlugin.id`, `capabilities.chatTypes`,
   and `outbound.base.textChunkLimit` are correct in `channel.test.ts`.

5. **Manifest invariant test** (if present in the repo): verify the `openclaw.plugin.json` id
   aligns with `package.json` openclaw.channel.id and the `defineChannelPluginEntry` id by
   running `pnpm test -- plugin-invariant` or similar.

6. **Manual registration check**: `pnpm openclaw channels status` should list `pulseboard`
   as a known channel (even if unconfigured) once the plugin is wired into the workspace.

content.text:
Here's a summary of the research findings and the plan I've prepared:

---

## Plan: PulseBoard Channel Plugin

**24 new files** exclusively under `extensions/pulseboard/` — no modifications to any existing files.

### Directory structure
```
extensions/pulseboard/
├── openclaw.plugin.json
├── package.json
├── index.ts
├── setup-entry.ts
├── api.ts
└── src/
    ├── types.ts, config-schema.ts, config-ui-hints.ts
    ├── accounts.ts + accounts.test.ts
    ├── runtime.ts, runtime-api.ts
    ├── channel.ts + channel.test.ts
    ├── client.ts
    ├── send.ts + send.test.ts
    ├── inbound.ts + inbound.test.ts
    ├── monitor.ts, normalize.ts, probe.ts
    ├── setup-core.ts, setup-surface.ts
```

### Key design decisions

1. **IRC as structural model** — channel.ts, accounts.ts, monitor.ts, inbound.ts all mirror IRC's patterns with PulseBoard-specific substitutions (`#board-name` for groups, username for DMs, API token auth).

2. **SDK import path for generic runtime helpers** — Each bundled plugin uses a channel-specific `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<id>` facade. Since `src/plugin-sdk/pulseboard.ts` cannot be created (outside the extension directory), `src/runtime-api.ts` imports `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `createChannelPairingController`, `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`, `chunkTextForOutbound`, and policy helpers from **`openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk`** — the only existing channel plugin that exports the complete set of these generic helpers. The plan notes to verify exact exports against `src/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk.ts` before writing that barrel.

3. **PulseBoard-specific values**: `textChunkLimit: 4000`, `chatTypes: ["direct", "group"]`, `media: false`, `blockStreaming: true`, token via `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` env or `apiToken` config, fictional HTTP long-polling transport in `client.ts`.

4. **4 test files** covering account resolution, send paths, all 5 inbound policy gate paths (open DM, allowlist drop, pairing challenge, board dispatch, mention gate), and plugin shape smoke tests.

---

## Plan: PulseBoard Channel Plugin

**24 new files** exclusively under `extensions/pulseboard/` — no modifications to any existing files.

### Directory structure
```
extensions/pulseboard/
├── openclaw.plugin.json
├── package.json
├── index.ts
├── setup-entry.ts
├── api.ts
└── src/
    ├── types.ts, config-schema.ts, config-ui-hints.ts

    ├── runtime.ts, runtime-api.ts
    ├── channel.ts + channel.test.ts
    ├── client.ts
    ├── send.ts + send.test.ts
    ├── inbound.ts + inbound.test.ts
    ├── monitor.ts, normalize.ts, probe.ts
    ├── setup-core.ts, setup-surface.ts
```

### Key design decisions

1. **IRC as structural model** — channel.ts, accounts.ts, monitor.ts, inbound.ts all mirror IRC's patterns with PulseBoard-specific substitutions (`#board-name` for groups, username for DMs, API token auth).

2. **SDK import path for generic runtime helpers** — Each bundled plugin uses a channel-specific `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<id>` facade. Since `src/plugin-sdk/pulseboard.ts` cannot be created (outside the extension directory), `src/runtime-api.ts` imports `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `createChannelPairingController`, `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`, `chunkTextForOutbound`, and policy helpers from **`openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk`** — the only existing channel plugin that exports the complete set of these generic helpers. The plan notes to verify exact exports against `src/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk.ts` before writing that barrel.

3. **PulseBoard-specific values**: `textChunkLimit: 4000`, `chatTypes: ["direct", "group"]`, `media: false`, `blockStreaming: true`, token via `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` env or `apiToken` config, fictional HTTP long-polling transport in `client.ts`.

4. **4 test files** covering account resolution, send paths, all 5 inbound policy gate paths (open DM, allowlist drop, pairing challenge, board dispatch, mention gate), and plugin shape smoke tests.

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: PulseBoard Channel Plugin

## Context

Add a new fictional "PulseBoard" channel plugin to OpenClaw. PulseBoard is an imaginary
real-time bulletin board messaging platform where users post to named "boards" (group chats,
identified with `#board-name`) and can also exchange direct messages (identified by plain
username). The plugin follows the same extension architecture as existing bundled channel plugins
(IRC, Nextcloud Talk, Telegram) and must satisfy:

- Both `"direct"` and `"group"` chat types
- 4 000-character text chunk limit
- Token-based authentication (`PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` env var or `apiToken` config field)
- HTTP long-polling for inbound messages (fictional transport)
- DM pairing flow, DM/group policy gating, security warnings
- No modifications to any existing file — only new files under `extensions/pulseboard/`

### Key architectural decision: SDK import path for generic channel runtime helpers

Every bundled channel plugin uses a channel-specific `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<id>` facade for
generic runtime helpers (`dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `createChannelPairingController`,
`deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`, `chunkTextForOutbound`, etc.).

Because `src/plugin-sdk/pulseboard.ts` cannot be created (it is outside the extension
directory), PulseBoard's private `runtime-api.ts` barrel imports those same helpers from
`openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk`, which exports the complete set of generic channel
runtime helpers. Additional helpers not re-exported there are pulled directly from their
own narrow SDK subpaths (e.g. `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers`,
`openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers`).

---

## File Tree

```
extensions/pulseboard/
├── openclaw.plugin.json          # Minimal manifest
├── package.json                  # npm metadata + openclaw channel block
├── index.ts                      # defineChannelPluginEntry
├── setup-entry.ts                # defineSetupPluginEntry
├── api.ts                        # Public barrel (accounts + setup-surface)
└── src/
    ├── types.ts                  # PulseBoardAccountConfig, CoreConfig, InboundMessage, Probe
    ├── config-schema.ts          # Zod schemas + buildChannelConfigSchema
    ├── config-ui-hints.ts        # UI hints for the config form
    ├── accounts.ts               # resolveAccount, listAccountIds, ResolvedPulseBoardAccount
    ├── accounts.test.ts          # Unit tests for account resolution
    ├── runtime.ts                # createPluginRuntimeStore → get/setPulseBoardRuntime
    ├── runtime-api.ts            # Private barrel (re-exports from openclaw/plugin-sdk/*)
    ├── channel.ts                # createChatChannelPlugin → pulseBoardPlugin export
    ├── channel.test.ts           # Smoke test: plugin shape + capabilities
    ├── client.ts                 # Stub PulseBoard HTTP client (connect, send, poll, disconnect)
    ├── send.ts                   # sendMessagePulseBoard + SendPulseBoardResult
    ├── send.test.ts              # Unit tests for send (happy + error paths)
    ├── inbound.ts                # handlePulseBoardInbound (DM/board policy gates + dispatch)
    ├── inbound.test.ts           # Unit tests for policy gates and dispatch paths
    ├── monitor.ts                # monitorPulseBoardProvider (poll loop + inbound hand-off)
    ├── normalize.ts              # normalizePulseBoardTarget, isBoardTarget, normalizeAllowEntry
    ├── probe.ts                  # probePulseBoard (connectivity check)
    ├── setup-core.ts             # pulseBoardSetupAdapter (resolveAccountId, applyAccountConfig)
    └── setup-surface.ts          # pulseBoardSetupWizard (credentials + allowFrom + dmPolicy)
```

Total: **24 new files** (5 root + 19 under `src/`).

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — `openclaw.plugin.json`

Minimal manifest. The actual per-account config schema is defined in code via
`buildChannelConfigSchema`; the top-level `configSchema` is intentionally empty.

```json
{
  "id": "pulseboard",
  "channels": ["pulseboard"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

---

### Step 2 — `package.json`

Follow IRC and Nextcloud Talk conventions. Key fields: name `@openclaw/pulseboard`, `type:
"module"`, the `openclaw` block with `extensions`, `setupEntry`, `install.minHostVersion`,
and the `channel` sub-object.

```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/pulseboard",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw PulseBoard channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "devDependencies": { "openclaw": "workspace:*" },
  "peerDependencies": { "openclaw": ">=2026.4.1" },
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "install": { "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1" },
    "channel": {
      "id": "pulseboard",
      "label": "PulseBoard",
      "selectionLabel": "PulseBoard (API Token)",
      "detailLabel": "PulseBoard",
      "docsPath": "/channels/pulseboard",
      "docsLabel": "pulseboard",
      "blurb": "real-time bulletin board with DM and board-based group messaging.",
      "aliases": ["pulse-board", "pb"],
      "systemImage": "list.bulletin",
      "quickstartAllowFrom": true
    }
  }
}
```

---

### Step 3 — `index.ts`

Entry point. Mirrors `extensions/irc/index.ts` exactly — wraps the plugin object with
`defineChannelPluginEntry`, re-exports the plugin and runtime setter for core / tests.

```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setPulseBoardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setPulseBoardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "pulseboard",
  name: "PulseBoard",
  description: "PulseBoard channel plugin",
  plugin: pulseBoardPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setPulseBoardRuntime,
});
```

---

### Step 4 — `setup-entry.ts`

One-liner setup entry. Identical pattern to every other bundled plugin.

```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin);
```

---

### Step 5 — `api.ts`

Public barrel. Exposes account helpers and setup surface for core / tests that need them
without importing internal `src/**` paths directly.

```typescript
export * from "./src/accounts.js";
export * from "./src/setup-surface.js";
```

---

### Step 6 — `src/types.ts`

Define all TypeScript types for the plugin. Key types:

- **`PulseBoardBoardConfig`** — per-board overrides (`requireMention?`, `allowFrom?`,
  `tools?`, `systemPrompt?`)
- **`PulseBoardAccountConfig`** — full account-level config shape:
  `apiToken?`, `tokenFile?`, `dmPolicy?`, `allowFrom?`, `boardPolicy?`,
  `boards?` (string[]), `boardConfig?` (Record<string, PulseBoardBoardConfig>),
  `defaultTo?`, `textChunkLimit?`, `name?`, `enabled?`
- **`PulseBoardConfig`** extends `PulseBoardAccountConfig` with `accounts?` and
  `defaultAccount?`
- **`CoreConfig`** — `OpenClawConfig & { channels?: { pulseboard?: PulseBoardConfig } }`
- **`PulseBoardInboundMessage`** — `{ messageId, boardName, senderUsername, text,
  timestamp, isBoard }` (analogous to `IrcInboundMessage`)
- **`PulseBoardProbe`** — `BaseProbeResult<string> & { apiUrl?: string; latencyMs?: number }`

Import `OpenClawConfig`, `DmPolicy`, `GroupPolicy`, `BaseProbeResult`, etc. from
`"./runtime-api.js"`.

---

### Step 7 — `src/config-schema.ts`

Build the Zod validation schema using helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema`.
Pattern: define `PulseBoardBoardSchema`, `PulseBoardAccountSchemaBase`, `PulseBoardAccountSchema`
(with `superRefine` for `requireOpenAllowFrom`), `PulseBoardConfigSchema` (adds `accounts` +
`defaultAccount`), then export `PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema` via `buildChannelConfigSchema`.

Key schema fields to include:
- `apiToken: z.string().optional()`
- `tokenFile: z.string().optional()`
- `dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing")`
- `allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional()`
- `boardPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist")`
- `boards: z.array(z.string()).optional()`
- `boardConfig: z.record(z.string(), PulseBoardBoardSchema.optional()).optional()`
- `...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape`

Imports: `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `GroupPolicySchema`,
`ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape`, `ToolPolicySchema`, `requireOpenAllowFrom`,
`MarkdownConfigSchema` from `"openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema"`;
`z` from `"openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod"`.

---

### Step 8 — `src/config-ui-hints.ts`

Export a `pulseBoardChannelConfigUiHints` object consumed by `buildChannelConfigSchema`.
Include hint entries for `apiToken` (sensitive, label "PulseBoard API token"),
`tokenFile` (label "Token file path"), `allowFrom`, and `boards`.
Pattern: copy the shape from `extensions/irc/src/config-ui-hints.ts`.

---

### Step 9 — `src/accounts.ts`

**This is the most functionally important file.**

Exports:
- `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount` type (fields: `accountId`, `enabled`, `name?`, `configured`,
  `apiToken`, `tokenSource: "env"|"tokenFile"|"config"|"none"`, `config: PulseBoardAccountConfig`)
- `listPulseBoardAccountIds` + `resolveDefaultPulseBoardAccountId` via
  `createAccountListHelpers("pulseboard", { normalizeAccountId })`
- `resolvePulseBoardAccount({ cfg, accountId? })` — merges account config via
  `resolveMergedAccountConfig`, resolves token with priority:
  1. `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` env var (default account only)
  2. `tokenFile` (via `tryReadSecretFileSync`)
  3. `apiToken` config field (via `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString`)
  4. `"none"` fallback
- `listEnabledPulseBoardAccounts(cfg)` — filters enabled accounts

SDK imports: `createAccountListHelpers` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers`;
`DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id`;
`resolveMergedAccountConfig` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-resolution`;
`tryReadSecretFileSync` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`;
`normalizeResolvedSecretInputString` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input`.

---

### Step 10 — `src/runtime.ts`

Simple runtime store, identical in shape to `extensions/irc/src/runtime.ts`.

```typescript
import { createPluginRuntimeStore } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store";
import type { PluginRuntime } from "./runtime-api.js";

const { setRuntime: setPulseBoardRuntime, getRuntime: getPulseBoardRuntime } =
  createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized");
export { getPulseBoardRuntime, setPulseBoardRuntime };
export function clearPulseBoardRuntime() {
  setPulseBoardRuntime(undefined as unknown as PluginRuntime);
}
```

---

### Step 11 — `src/runtime-api.ts`

**Critical design note:** Because no `src/plugin-sdk/pulseboard.ts` can be created, this
barrel imports generic channel runtime helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk`
(which exports the full set: `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `createChannelPairingController`,
`deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`, `chunkTextForOutbound`, policy helpers, etc.).

During implementation, verify the exact export list by reading
`src/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk.ts`. For any helper not found there, fall back to its
own narrow SDK subpath (e.g. `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers` for status utilities).

```typescript
// Private runtime barrel for the bundled PulseBoard extension.
export {
  buildBaseChannelStatusSummary,
  createAccountStatusSink,
  chunkTextForOutbound,
  createChannelPairingController,
  DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID,
  deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments,
  dispatchInboundReplyWithBase,
  getChatChannelMeta,
  GROUP_POLICY_BLOCKED_LABEL,
  logInboundDrop,
  PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
  readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy,
  resolveAllowlistProviderRuntimeGroupPolicy,
  resolveControlCommandGate,
  resolveDefaultGroupPolicy,
  resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists,
  warnMissingProviderGroupPolicyFallbackOnce,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk";
export type {
  BaseProbeResult,
  ChannelPlugin,
  DmPolicy,
  GroupPolicy,
  OpenClawConfig,
  OutboundReplyPayload,
  PluginRuntime,
  RuntimeEnv,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk";
```

---

### Step 12 — `src/normalize.ts`

Target and allow-entry normalization helpers.

- **`isBoardTarget(target)`** — returns `true` if target starts with `#`
- **`normalizePulseBoardTarget(raw)`** — lowercases, strips whitespace; boards keep `#` prefix,
  usernames strip `@` prefix; returns `undefined` for invalid/empty inputs
- **`looksLikePulseBoardTargetId(raw)`** — quick heuristic for the `targetResolver.looksLikeId`
  hint (returns true if valid board or username pattern)
- **`normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry(entry)`** — normalize usernames for the allowlist (strip `@`,
  lowercase)

---

### Step 13 — `src/client.ts`

Stub PulseBoard HTTP client (fictional transport). Realistic enough that send/monitor can
call it, but backed by placeholder fetch calls rather than a real library.

```typescript
export type PulseBoardClientOptions = {
  apiToken: string;
  apiUrl?: string;           // default: "https://api.pulseboard.example"
  boards?: string[];         // boards to subscribe to
  abortSignal?: AbortSignal;
  onMessage?: (msg: PulseBoardRawMessage) => void | Promise<void>;
};

export type PulseBoardRawMessage = {
  id: string;
  boardName?: string;        // set for board messages
  senderUsername: string;
  text: string;
  timestamp: number;
  isDirect: boolean;
};

export type PulseBoardClient = {
  isReady(): boolean;
  sendMessage(to: string, text: string): Promise<{ messageId: string }>;
  disconnect(): void;
};

export async function connectPulseBoardClient(
  opts: PulseBoardClientOptions,
): Promise<PulseBoardClient> { ... }
```

The implementation simulates polling via `setInterval` / `fetch` (stub — never actually
reaches a server), records `isReady = true` once connected, and calls `onMessage` for each
simulated or received message. `disconnect()` clears the interval and sets `isReady = false`.

---

### Step 14 — `src/send.ts`

Implements `sendMessagePulseBoard(to, text, opts)`.

```typescript
export type SendPulseBoardOptions = {
  cfg?: CoreConfig;
  accountId?: string;
  replyTo?: string;
  client?: PulseBoardClient;
};

export type SendPulseBoardResult = {
  messageId: string;
  target: string;
};

export async function sendMessagePulseBoard(
  to: string,
  text: string,
  opts: SendPulseBoardOptions = {},
): Promise<SendPulseBoardResult>
```

Logic:
1. Resolve account via `resolvePulseBoardAccount`
2. Guard: `account.configured` required
3. Normalize target via `normalizePulseBoardTarget`
4. Prepend/append `[reply:${replyTo}]` suffix if `opts.replyTo` is set
5. Use provided `opts.client` if ready, else open a transient `connectPulseBoardClient`
6. Call `client.sendMessage(target, payload)`
7. Record outbound activity via runtime `channel.activity.record`
8. Return `{ messageId, target }`

---

### Step 15 — `src/probe.ts`

```typescript
export async function probePulseBoard(
  cfg: CoreConfig,
  opts?: { accountId?: string | null; timeoutMs?: number },
): Promise<PulseBoardProbe>
```

Makes a lightweight API connectivity check (e.g. `GET /v1/ping` with the auth token),
records latency, and returns a `PulseBoardProbe` with `ok`, `message`, and `latencyMs`.
Gracefully handles network errors and returns `ok: false` with the error message.

---

### Step 16 — `src/inbound.ts`

Handles all inbound message policy gating and dispatch. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/inbound.ts`
closely.

```typescript
export async function handlePulseBoardInbound(params: {
  message: PulseBoardInboundMessage;
  account: ResolvedPulseBoardAccount;
  config: CoreConfig;
  runtime: RuntimeEnv;
  sendReply?: (to: string, text: string, replyToId?: string) => Promise<void>;
  statusSink?: (patch: { lastInboundAt?: number; lastOutboundAt?: number }) => void;
}): Promise<void>
```

Logic (mirrors IRC inbound, adapted for PulseBoard):
1. Guard empty body
2. Create `ChannelPairingController` via `createChannelPairingController`
3. Resolve `dmPolicy` (default `"pairing"`) and `boardPolicy` (default `"allowlist"`)
4. Resolve effective allowlists via `resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists`
5. **If board message (`isBoard`)**:
   - Check board-level group access gate
   - Check per-sender allowlist
6. **If DM**:
   - If `dmPolicy === "disabled"` → drop
   - If not `"open"` and sender not in allowlist → issue pairing challenge or drop
7. Check control command gate via `resolveControlCommandGate`
8. Check mention gate (boards: require `@bot` mention unless `requireMention=false`)
9. Resolve agent route via `core.channel.routing.resolveAgentRoute`
10. Build `ctxPayload`:
    - `From`: `"pulseboard:#board-name"` or `"pulseboard:@username"`
    - `To`: board name or username
    - `ChatType`: `"group"` or `"direct"`
    - `Provider/Surface`: `"pulseboard"`
    - `GroupSubject`: board name (for board messages)
11. Dispatch via `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, delivering reply via
    `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments` → `sendMessagePulseBoard`

---

### Step 17 — `src/monitor.ts`

```typescript
export async function monitorPulseBoardProvider(opts: {
  accountId?: string;
  config?: CoreConfig;
  runtime?: RuntimeEnv;
  abortSignal?: AbortSignal;
  statusSink?: (patch: { lastInboundAt?: number; lastOutboundAt?: number }) => void;
}): Promise<{ stop: () => void }>
```

Logic:
1. Resolve account, guard `configured`
2. Create logger child
3. Call `connectPulseBoardClient({ apiToken, boards, abortSignal, onMessage })`
4. In `onMessage`: build `PulseBoardInboundMessage`, record inbound activity, call
   `handlePulseBoardInbound`
5. Return `{ stop: () => client.disconnect() }`

Used by `channel.ts` gateway `startAccount` via `runStoppablePassiveMonitor`.

---

### Step 18 — `src/setup-core.ts`

Exports `pulseBoardSetupAdapter: ChannelSetupAdapter` using the standard
`createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`-derived helpers.

Key methods:
- `resolveAccountId`: maps setup token input → account id (default `"default"`)
- `applyAccountConfig`: writes `apiToken` / `tokenFile` / `allowFrom` / `dmPolicy` into
  `cfg.channels.pulseboard`
- `validateInput`: checks token is non-empty
- `applyAccountName`: sets `channels.pulseboard.name`

---

### Step 19 — `src/setup-surface.ts`

Exports `pulseBoardSetupWizard: ChannelSetupWizard`.

Wizard sections (modeled on `extensions/telegram/src/setup-surface.ts`):
1. **Token credential** — prompt for API token; check `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` env var;
   `sensitive: true`; `preferredEnvVar: "PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN"`
2. **DM Policy** — `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicy` helper
3. **Allow-from** — `createAllowFromSection` with `idLabel: "PulseBoard username"`

---

### Step 20 — `src/channel.ts`

The central plugin definition. Creates `pulseBoardPlugin` via `createChatChannelPlugin`.

```typescript
export const pulseBoardPlugin: ChannelPlugin<ResolvedPulseBoardAccount, PulseBoardProbe> =
  createChatChannelPlugin({
    base: {
      id: "pulseboard",
      meta: { ...getChatChannelMeta("pulseboard"), quickstartAllowFrom: true },
      setup: pulseBoardSetupAdapter,
      setupWizard: pulseBoardSetupWizard,
      capabilities: {
        chatTypes: ["direct", "group"],
        media: false,            // stub client; no media upload
        blockStreaming: true,
      },
      reload: { configPrefixes: ["channels.pulseboard"] },
      configSchema: PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema,
      config: {
        ...pulseBoardConfigAdapter,   // createScopedChannelConfigAdapter
        isConfigured: (account) => account.configured,
        describeAccount: (account) => describeAccountSnapshot({ account, configured: account.configured, extra: { tokenSource: account.tokenSource } }),
      },
      groups: {
        resolveRequireMention: ...,   // reads boardConfig[groupId]?.requireMention
        resolveToolPolicy: ...,
      },
      messaging: {
        normalizeTarget: normalizePulseBoardTarget,
        targetResolver: { looksLikeId: looksLikePulseBoardTargetId, hint: "<#board|username>" },
      },
      resolver: { resolveTargets: ... },
      directory: createChannelDirectoryAdapter({ listPeers: ..., listGroups: ... }),
      status: createComputedAccountStatusAdapter({
        defaultRuntime: createDefaultChannelRuntimeState(DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID),
        buildChannelSummary: ...,
        probeAccount: async ({ cfg, account, timeoutMs }) =>
          probePulseBoard(cfg as CoreConfig, { accountId: account.accountId, timeoutMs }),
        resolveAccountSnapshot: ...,
      }),
      gateway: {
        startAccount: async (ctx) => {
          // guard configured, log, runStoppablePassiveMonitor → monitorPulseBoardProvider
        },
      },
    },
    pairing: {
      text: {
        idLabel: "pulseboardUser",
        message: PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
        normalizeAllowEntry: normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry,
        notify: async ({ id, message }) => {
          const target = normalizePulseBoardTarget(id);
          if (!target) throw new Error(`invalid PulseBoard pairing id: ${id}`);
          await sendMessagePulseBoard(target, message);
        },
      },
    },
    security: {
      resolveDmPolicy: createScopedDmSecurityResolver({ channelKey: "pulseboard", ... }),
      collectWarnings: composeAccountWarningCollectors(collectPulseBoardGroupPolicyWarnings),
    },
    outbound: {
      base: {
        deliveryMode: "direct",
        chunker: chunkTextForOutbound,
        chunkerMode: "markdown",
        textChunkLimit: 4000,        // PulseBoard spec
      },
      attachedResults: {
        channel: "pulseboard",
        sendText: async ({ cfg, to, text, accountId, replyToId }) =>
          sendMessagePulseBoard(to, text, { cfg: cfg as CoreConfig, accountId: accountId ?? undefined, replyTo: replyToId ?? undefined }),
        sendMedia: async ({ cfg, to, text, mediaUrl, accountId, replyToId }) =>
          sendMessagePulseBoard(to, mediaUrl ? `${text}\n\nAttachment: ${mediaUrl}` : text, { ... }),
      },
    },
```

content.text:
Here are the complete contents of the files you requested:

## 1. **extensions/irc/src/probe.ts**

This file provides IRC connectivity probing functionality:
- `probeIrc()` - Connects to an IRC server with a timeout to verify connectivity
- Takes a `CoreConfig` and optional `accountId` and `timeoutMs` parameters
- Returns an `IrcProbe` object with connection status, latency, and error details
- Handles errors gracefully and formats them as strings
- Default timeout is 8000ms

## 2. **extensions/irc/src/policy.ts**

This file manages IRC channel access control policies with several key functions:

- `resolveIrcGroupMatch()` - Matches IRC channels against configured groups/allowlists (case-insensitive matching with wildcard support)
- `resolveIrcGroupAccessGate()` - Determines if a channel is accessible based on policy ("allowlist", "open", or "disabled")
- `resolveIrcRequireMention()` - Resolves whether mentions are required (per-channel config with wildcard fallback, defaults to true)
- `resolveIrcMentionGate()` - Decides if a message should be skipped based on mention requirements and authorization
- `resolveIrcGroupSenderAllowed()` - Checks if a sender is on the allowlist, checking inner allowlist first, then outer, with policy fallback

## 3. **extensions/irc/src/normalize.ts**

This file provides normalization and validation utilities:

- `isChannelTarget()` - Checks if target starts with `#` or `&`
- `normalizeIrcMessagingTarget()` - Parses raw target strings with support for prefixes like `irc:`, `channel:`, `user:`
- `looksLikeIrcTargetId()` - Validates IRC target format (no control chars, matches pattern)
- `normalizeIrcAllowEntry()` - Normalizes allowlist entries (trim, lowercase, strip prefixes)
- `normalizeIrcAllowlist()` - Normalizes arrays of allowlist entries
- `formatIrcSenderId()` - Formats sender ID from message (nick, user, host components)
- `buildIrcAllowlistCandidates()` - Generates matching candidates from message sender info (nick, user@host combinations)
- `resolveIrcAllowlistMatch()` - Matches sender against allowlist with wildcard and candidate matching support

## 4. **extensions/irc/src/channel-config-api.ts**

This file does **not exist** in the repository.

## 5. **extensions/irc/src/connect-options.ts** (first 30 lines)

This file builds IRC connection options from account configuration:
- `buildIrcConnectOptions()` - Creates `IrcClientOptions` from a `ResolvedIrcAccount` with support for connection overrides
- Maps account host, port, TLS, nick, username, realname, password
- Handles nickserv configuration (enabled, service, password, register, registerEmail)
- Supports partial overrides while maintaining core connection parameters

---

## Key Patterns for PulseBoard Channel Plugin

Based on these files, the IRC plugin uses these patterns you may want to replicate:

1. **Policy Resolution Chain**: Multiple resolver functions that compose to determine access (match → gate → mention → sender checks)
2. **Normalized Configuration Matching**: Case-insensitive matching with wildcards and fallback chains
3. **Allowlist Matching**: Multi-candidate matching (nick, user@host combinations) against allowlists
4. **Graceful Error Handling**: Try-catch with formatted error returns instead of throwing
5. **Configuration Inheritance**: Per-channel config with wildcard fallback patterns
6. **Target Normalization**: Support multiple input formats with prefix stripping

content.text:
Only `extensions/pulseboard/` appears as untracked — no existing files were modified. Here's a complete summary of what was implemented:

---

## PulseBoard Channel Plugin — Complete

**24 new files** created exclusively under `extensions/pulseboard/`. Zero existing files modified.

### File manifest

```
extensions/pulseboard/
├── openclaw.plugin.json      Minimal manifest (id: pulseboard, channels: [pulseboard])
├── package.json              @openclaw/pulseboard, openclaw.channel metadata, version 2026.4.1-beta.1
├── index.ts                  defineChannelPluginEntry → pulseBoardPlugin
├── setup-entry.ts            defineSetupPluginEntry
├── api.ts                    Public barrel: accounts + setup-surface
└── src/
    ├── types.ts              PulseBoardAccountConfig, PulseBoardConfig, CoreConfig,
    │                         PulseBoardInboundMessage, PulseBoardProbe
    ├── runtime-api.ts        Private SDK barrel: openclaw/plugin-sdk/nextcloud-talk
    │                         (dispatch, pairing, policy helpers) +
    │                         openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc (chunkTextForOutbound,
    │                         getChatChannelMeta, PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
    │                         resolveControlCommandGate, resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists)
    ├── runtime.ts            createPluginRuntimeStore → get/setPulseBoardRuntime
    ├── config-ui-hints.ts    UI hints for apiToken, tokenFile, dmPolicy, boardPolicy, boards
    ├── config-schema.ts      Zod schema via buildChannelConfigSchema; PulseBoardBoardSchema,
    │                         PulseBoardConfigSchema with requireOpenAllowFrom validation
    ├── normalize.ts          normalizePulseBoardTarget (#board / username),
    │                         isBoardTarget, looksLikePulseBoardTargetId,
    │                         normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry/Allowlist,
    │                         matchesPulseBoardAllowlist
    ├── accounts.ts           resolvePulseBoardAccount (env → tokenFile → config priority),
    │                         listPulseBoardAccountIds, listEnabledPulseBoardAccounts
    ├── client.ts             Stub PulseBoardClient (connect, sendMessage, disconnect, poll loop)
    ├── probe.ts              probePulseBoard — GET /v1/ping with latency measurement
    ├── send.ts               sendMessagePulseBoard — resolves account, normalizes target,
    │                         appends [reply:id] suffix, uses transient client if needed
    ├── setup-core.ts         pulseBoardSetupAdapter (resolveAccountId, validateInput,
    │                         applyAccountConfig, applyAccountName)
    ├── setup-surface.ts      pulseBoardSetupWizard (token credential step,
    │                         dmPolicy section, allowFrom section)
    ├── inbound.ts            handlePulseBoardInbound — full policy gate chain:
    │                         board access → DM allowlist/pairing → command gate →
    │                         mention gate → dispatchInboundReplyWithBase
    ├── monitor.ts            monitorPulseBoardProvider — connectPulseBoardClient,
    │                         message → PulseBoardInboundMessage, handlePulseBoardInbound
    ├── channel.ts            pulseBoardPlugin via createChatChannelPlugin:
    │                         chatTypes [direct, group], textChunkLimit 4000,
    │                         media false, blockStreaming true
    ├── accounts.test.ts      Token resolution priority, env var, enabled flag, accountId
    ├── send.test.ts          Happy path, DM/@-prefix strip, reply suffix, error cases
    ├── inbound.test.ts       5 policy gate paths: open DM, allowlist drop, pairing challenge,
    │                         board dispatch, requireMention gate
    └── channel.test.ts       Plugin shape smoke: id, chatTypes, textChunkLimit, pairing.idLabel
```
